evidence_basis_summary: Paper assumes three pre-curated labeled datasets (BFRS/CCC/Manifestos) and does not address study/material inclusion decisions; no adjudication of analytic universe appears.
notes: Upgraded low to high: full text confirms role is not applicable in this measurement-focused study.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper concerns measurement validity (Adcock and Collier) not causal mechanism; downstream-inference bias is only cross-referenced to Egami et al. 2023.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct full text confirms scope is measurement not mechanism.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper's core claim is that codebook operationalization (e.g., protest including/excluding strikes; CAMEO vs CCC) cannot be bypassed or automated and humans must systematize constructs.
notes: Confirmed at high via Stage 0 language and protest/strikes example.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors collect three diverse codebook corpora, but the work is about measurement on each, not about keeping the evidence base from collapsing onto a machine-friendly corpus.
notes: Upgraded low to high: role definition does not match paper's diversity-of-datasets move.

evidence_basis_summary: Five-stage framework, seven behavioral tests, semi-structured machine-readable codebook format, open-weight choice to ensure reproducibility, Dataverse replication, and SI data-provenance section explicitly design admissible procedure.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable no to yes: role is directly and centrally performed by the authors (framework, tests, versioning, replication).

evidence_basis_summary: Evaluation is within-dataset train/dev/test splits; no cross-population transport or simulated-to-real transfer claim is adjudicated.
notes: Upgraded low to high: full text confirms no transportability adjudication.

evidence_basis_summary: Study is purely text classification on archival news/manifesto corpora; no interview, field, or situated-interpretation component.
notes: Upgraded low to high via full-text confirmation.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors themselves perform manual error analysis and recommend applied researchers do so; open-weight/replication choices imply accountability, but responsibility allocation is not the paper's focus.
notes: Upgraded low to medium and observable no to partial: accountability is implied via author-done manual analysis and reproducibility stance, not directly theorized.

evidence_basis_summary: Codebook preparation (Stage 0) with label, definition, clarification, negative clarification, positive/negative examples is the exact category-boundary work described in this split.
notes: Confirmed high; exclusion rules (e.g.,  welfare_positive excludes education; riot excludes police) are clear boundary work.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper works with single gold labels and does not preserve subgroup/annotator disagreement; inter-coder work is limited to order-invariance across LLM runs (Test IV), not human pluralism.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable partial to no / status unclear to not_applicable: full text shows no representational-pluralism design.

evidence_basis_summary: No cross-setting transport test; evaluation stays within each codebook's own dev set.
No simulator calibration or matching to an external reference distribution; F1 is reported against held-out human labels with a majority-class baseline.
No interviewer/respondent interaction; inputs are archival news articles and party manifestos.
No tacit/archival-context interpretation by humans is built into the workflow beyond codebook authoring, which is captured by the construct boundary role.
No title/abstract triage or early study ranking; the task is document-level classification into codebook categories.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Zero-shot LLMs match some but not all human labels; instruction-tuning lifts BFRS F1 from 0.53 to 0.82 (+55%), showing human coders shift toward auditor/labeler roles only when labels are high-consensus and training data exist.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct quantitative evidence (Table 6; Mistral-7B zero-shot errors on Manifestos at 45% non-compliance) supports conditional shrinking.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper does not involve LLM drafting/polishing of prose; LLMs are used solely as classifiers over codebook-defined labels.
